summary: Four proposals are mathematically viable for Phase 3 eval (smart_crowd_divergence_long, realized_vol_expansion_filter, btc_decorrelation_alpha_filter, basis_normalization_exit); two require concrete field disambiguation before implementation (taker_thin_book_reversal_short, cross_channel_conflict_suppressor); two should be rejected outright — burst_oi_momentum_long for too_few samples and unresolvable wildcard fields, cross_channel_conflict_suppressor for zero evaluable triggers under standard harness scoring.

analyses.distinctness_notes: E001 conditions on oi_change_pct alone as a single-dim OI crowding threshold. This archetype requires simultaneous satisfaction of premium_bps >2σ AND funding>0 AND OI rising — a triple-conjunction over three different data streams. No existing OI_Price short archetype implements this joint structure. Structurally distinct, though the delta from E001 is additive threshold conditions rather than a fundamentally different signal family; a strict reading could argue it is E001 + 2 extra conditions, but the information sources (premium, funding, OI) are sufficiently orthogonal to qualify as distinct.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: 3000 OI_Price events × 0.15 (premium_bps >2σ tail rate, by definition ~15% of observations exceed 2σ) × 0.50 (funding>0 base rate on perps, funding is positive majority of bull regimes) × 0.40 (OI rising at event time, conservative) = 90
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Pairs most naturally with basis_normalization_exit (same mean-reversion thesis) and fixed TP/SL exits. ATR exits applicable. Funding rate is published at 8h cadence — observable at T0 but potentially stale relative to intra-minute premium_bps and oi_change_pct; this is a data-freshness concern, not a computability failure. Sample is tight at 90; recommend relaxing premium_bps threshold to 1.5σ in preliminary screen to confirm trigger count before full grid.

analyses.distinctness_notes: No existing archetype uses the divergence between top_trader_position_ratio and global_long_short_ratio as a joint entry signal. E001 and all known OI entries use raw OI magnitude or change; this adds an informed-vs-uninformed flow split derived from two separate Binance sentiment endpoints — a structurally different signal source. Distinct if and only if the lookback window for 'rising' and 'falling' is explicitly defined (currently underdetermined).
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: 7300 total cross-channel events (* fires on any channel) × 0.20 (simultaneous divergence: top_trader_position_ratio rising while global_long_short_ratio falling — conservatively 20% joint occurrence assuming partial negative correlation) × 0.35 (oi_change_5m > 0 in that context, conservatively) × 0.70 (long-side conviction filter, excluding ambiguous readings) = 360. Estimate has ±50% uncertainty until lookback window for 'rising/falling' is fixed.
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Long-side entry with no structural dependency on premium or basis fields, so all 100 exit archetypes are compatible. Fixed TP/SL, ATR, and trailing stop exits all apply without modification. Primary pre-implementation requirement: define explicit lookback window (e.g., 5m or 15m delta) for both ratio trends before optimizer sees the data, or the window itself becomes an overfit parameter. top_trader_position_ratio timestamp lag on Binance must be verified to be strictly < T0 to avoid implicit look-ahead.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Existing pricechange shorts condition on price momentum or magnitude metrics. This three-way conjunction of taker_buy_ratio_5m × liquidity_bucket × realized_vol_30m is a microstructure-regime entry targeting order-flow exhaustion in illiquid low-vol conditions — not present in any known pricechange archetype and structurally distinct from OI-based logic.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: 3500 pricechange events × 0.20 (taker_buy_ratio_5m > 0.70 rate, conservative — extreme taker imbalance is uncommon) × 0.15 (thin liquidity_bucket: thin books represent ~15% of symbol-time observations, dominated by low-volume alts) × 0.33 (low realized_vol_30m, bottom tercile) = 35
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Short-side mean-reversion entry; compatible with fixed TP/SL and ATR exits across all exit families. liquidity_bucket is the blocking issue: it is not a native Binance API field. If BWE precomputes it as a quantile bin of bid-ask spread or order-book depth, it becomes computable at T0 — confirm field exists in BWE feature registry before proceeding. With sample at 35 and thin-book slippage likely consuming the reversal edge, this archetype needs post-cost edge verification before Phase 3 entry.

analyses.distinctness_notes: All 100 existing exits are fixed TP/SL, ATR-based, or trailing stop families — all price-offset-from-entry triggers. This is the first condition-based exit using a market-state variable (basis reversion to rolling mean) as the primary exit trigger. Not expressible as a parameter delta of any existing exit; constitutes a genuinely new exit family.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: ~60 OI_Price entry archetypes × avg 5 triggers each per 30d = 300 premium-context open trades. Basis normalizes within trade duration in ~50% of mean-reversion cases = 150 meaningful exit triggers from relevant pairings. Non-premium entry pairings (pricechange, Reserved6) contribute near-zero evaluable signal since basis did not spike at entry — 'normalization within 1σ' is trivially satisfied or undefined in those cases. Effective evaluable sample is ~150.
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Only meaningful when paired with premium_bps- or basis-spiking entry archetypes (OI_Price family, and the proposed premium_funding_squeeze_short). Pairing with pricechange or Reserved6 entries produces an undefined or always-true exit condition since basis was not elevated at entry. Requires either explicit pairing restrictions in registry metadata or a graceful default (fall back to fixed SL) when basis was not anomalous at entry time. 30m rolling mean must use strictly causal window (expanding from session open or pre-entry data only) to avoid including the spike itself in the normalization denominator.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Existing 120 filters gate on session hour, weekday, btc_24h_pct, and macro event flags. None condition on intra-day realized_vol_30m regime direction (expanding vs contracting). Vol regime direction is orthogonal to all existing filter dimensions — a structurally new filter family. No existing archetype can produce this filter as a ≤3-line parameter delta.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: 7300 total events across all channels × 0.33 (top vol tercile, by definition) × 0.50 (expanding vs prior 30m window — vol expansion occurs roughly half the time when already in top tercile) = 1205 events pass filter per 30d
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Filter archetype — does not constrain exit family. All 100 exit archetypes remain fully compatible downstream. Critical caveat: cross-sectional vs per-symbol tercile definition is ambiguous in spec and changes the filter's character substantially (cross-sectional biases toward chronically high-vol symbols; per-symbol biases toward symbols currently in vol expansion regime). Spec must disambiguate. Additionally, applying this filter universally to E001 (mean-reversion short, validated keep) will exclude high-vol expanding regimes from E001 triggers, which may degrade the one confirmed strategy — recommend scoping filter to momentum-side entries or adding E001 exclusion in filter applicability metadata.

analyses.distinctness_notes: No existing filter uses btc_correlation_30d as a gate. Rolling correlation as a universe-selection dimension is structurally distinct from session, weekday, or macro-event filters. The 2D combination with market_cap_bucket adds a second orthogonal dimension not present in any current filter archetype. Distinct, but selectivity is low.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: 7300 total events × 0.50 (below-median btc_correlation_30d, by definition retains exactly 50% of symbols) × 0.50 (market_cap_bucket: mid/small cap assumption, conservatively 50% of actively traded universe on Binance futures) = 1825. Note: the 50% correlation retention is barely selective — true discriminatory power depends heavily on how market_cap_bucket is defined. If restricted to bottom cap quintile (~20%), effective retention drops to 7300 × 0.50 × 0.20 = 730, which is still ample but reduces the selectivity concern.
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Filter archetype — all 100 exit archetypes compatible. market_cap_bucket is not a native Binance API field; Binance exchange endpoints do not expose market capitalization. Requires CMC, CoinGecko, or equivalent external data source enriched at T0 per symbol. If replaced with a Binance-native proxy (e.g., 30d average notional volume quintile), the archetype becomes fully computable. btc_correlation_30d is computable from OHLCV price returns. Verify NaN handling for newly-listed symbols lacking 30d history — systematic exclusion of new listings may remove high-edge symbols.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Existing 60 cross-channel archetypes combine same-direction correlated signals for confluence amplification. This archetype detects directional conflict and suppresses — structurally inverted logic constituting a new functional family. Cannot be expressed as a parameter delta of any existing cross-channel archetype. Distinct in design intent, but has critical implementability failures.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: Suppressor produces zero positive triggers — it removes existing triggers. Estimated conflict events available to suppress: 7300 events × 0.25 (P(second channel fires within 90s opposing direction, rough estimate) × 0.60 (premium_bps filter pass) ≈ 1095 potential suppression events per 30d. However, standard val_score infrastructure measures triggered-trade outcomes; a suppressor's value is in avoided losses, requiring counterfactual evaluation. Effective evaluable triggers under current harness = 0.
uncomputable_fields: burst_count_* (wildcard field name — no specific field defined; requires disambiguation to e.g. burst_count_1m or burst_count_5m before implementation)

premium_bps cross-channel spread (semantically undefined — premium_bps is a per-symbol scalar, not a per-channel quantity; 'spread between two channels' has no clear definition in the Binance feature set)
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Suppressor archetype is structurally incompatible with standard entry→exit pipeline scoring. No exit can be paired with a non-triggered trade. Requires counterfactual evaluation infrastructure (avoided-loss metric) not confirmed in the current harness. Recommend deferring entirely until: (1) harness counterfactual support is verified, (2) wildcard field names are disambiguated, and (3) premium_bps cross-channel semantics are formally defined. Channel publish-latency skew across Reserved6 vs OI_Price will corrupt 90s conflict windows until timestamp normalization is implemented.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Reserved6 has 35 existing entries; generator asserts none use burst_count_* as primary signal. If accurate, treating message-burst frequency as an institutional accumulation proxy is a structurally different signal source from price or OI magnitude metrics. Distinctness conditionally confirmed, but cannot be fully verified without inspecting all 35 Reserved6 entry IDs. Wildcard field names are the primary blocker.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: 800 Reserved6 events × 0.20 (burst_count_* > 30m rolling P80 — by definition 20% of observations exceed the 80th percentile) × 0.45 (oi_change_5m > 0) × 0.35 (pretrend_* bullish, conservative for long-side) = 25. Temporal clustering due to burst autocorrelation (symbols stay in P80 regime for extended periods) likely reduces effective independent observations to <12, making the true evaluable sample well below the too_few threshold.
uncomputable_fields: burst_count_* (wildcard — no specific field name defined in Binance feature set; must resolve to a concrete field e.g. burst_count_5m)
pretrend_* (wildcard — no specific field name defined; requires disambiguation to concrete pretrend direction or strength field)
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Long-side momentum entry; all long-compatible exit archetypes apply in principle. However, the two wildcard field blockers and too_few sample count make exit pairing analysis premature. If burst_count_5m and a concrete pretrend field (e.g. pretrend_direction_15m) are the intended fields, resubmit with concrete names and rerun sample estimate — the underlying thesis is coherent but the spec is not implementable as written. Devil's temporal-clustering concern on effective sample size is well-founded and will persist even after field disambiguation.
